Messages: Hello, my name is Jordyn. I’m in high school, and my parents say this is supposed to be the most fun time of my life before I take on the responsibility of being an adult, but I’ve been seriously struggling. I struggle to make friends anywhere other than Discord, and seeing all of my classmates posting on instagram makes me feel worthless. I’m not doing very well in school, I don’t even think I’m going to graduate. I constantly feel anxious and depressed and I don’t know what to do. I’m beginning to realize that maybe constantly being online is affecting my health. Does anyone else feel this way? How can I feel better?

Hey Jordyn, this is Brock. I know how you feel, I’ve been experiencing a lot of the same things, and I’ve seen screen addiction ruin some of my friends lives. I had one friend who is no longer with us. It’s a shame because he was so bright and had such potential, but technology got in his way and clouded his mind, so he just wasn’t thinking right. Just know, it does get better. It always does. It’s impossible to change everything all at once, but everyday think about small things you could do to feel better. Maybe spend less time on the computer and try going for a walk. Maybe find something you enjoy doing that doesn’t involve a screen. It can be hard to try to do this yourself, just know there are so many people you can talk to about this and get support from. You should check out the get help page, it provides a lot of resources that were helpful to me.
